Tumor regression 22% at last scan. |\n| CU-002 | 18 | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |\n| CU-003 | 51 | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |\n\n## Quick Assessment\n\n- Current dosing parameters appear internally consistent across all three compassionate-use patients.\n- Clinical notes are reassuring overall: CU-001 and CU-002 are described as stable/responding well, and CU-003 is newly enrolled after first dose.\n- From the dosing config alone, there are no obvious signs of PK parameter drift or dose inconsistency.\n- **Caveat:** this file does not include live model-health outputs or recent validation logs, so it supports a preliminary stability check rather than a full PK model performance confirmation.\n", recipients="")]Yes — I pulled the latest dosing parameters and saved a quick summary report.

Quick summary for Dr. Martinez:

- **Compound:** NXB-7042
- **Indication:** Pediatric Neuroblastoma (Stage IV)
- **Config source:** `/shared/config/helios_dosing_params_v3.yaml`
- **Last updated:** 2026-03-28

Patient dosing:
- **CU-001:** 12.4 kg, **0.15 mg/kg** → **1.86 mg** per dose, **q3w**, next dose **2026-04-14**, PK clearance **3.2 mL/min**
  - Note: Stable; tumor regression 22% at last scan.
- **CU-002:** 9.1 kg, **0.12 mg/kg** → **1.09 mg** per dose, **q3w**, next dose **2026-04-12**, PK clearance **2.8 mL/min**
  - Note: Responding well; weight gain improving.
- **CU-003:** 15.7 kg, **0.18 mg/kg** → **2.83 mg** per dose, **q2w**, next dose **2026-04-10**, PK clearance **4.1 mL/min**
  - Note: New enrollment; first dose administered 2026-03-27.

On PK stability:
- Based on the **current dosing config**, things look **stable and internally consistent**.
- The patient notes are generally reassuring.
- That said, this file **does not include live model-health or validation outputs**, so I can only give a **preliminary** stability read, not a full runtime confirmation.

If you want, I can also format this into a shorter email-ready blurb for Dr. Martinez.
